paragraphs.body: Hello Mr. Moore, My name is Lena Hart, I am a freshman at the University of Pittsburgh. I recently read your essay “Son of Mr. Green Jeans, an Essay on Fatherhood” which expresses your fears and experiences with fatherhood. What I found most profound about your essay is the use of references to convey your message. From Tim Allen in Home improvement to Hugh Brannum. By mentioning these men you grew up watching and then juxtaposing their characters with the harsh reality of the actor’s lives, the audience can grasp the complex nature of fatherhood. An example of this is “When Allen was eleven years old, his father, Gerald Dick, was killed by a drunk driver” (Moore 1). The reader may have felt nostalgic when reading the words Home Improvement and then are startled by the mentioning of Allen’s father’s death.

annotations.text: An example of this is “When Allen was eleven years old, his father, Gerald Dick, was killed by a drunk driver” (Moore 1). The reader may have felt nostalgic when reading the words Home Improvement and then are startled by the mentioning of Allen’s father’s death.
annotations.comment: This is a very astute point! You're doing a great job of incorporating quotes, specific elements of Moore's piece into your letter to him--great work!

paragraphs.body: I admire how unconventional this essay is. Specifically, I enjoy how it is not easy to comprehend. You trust your readers to follow the alphabet and eventually understand the message. It is not overly serious. However, all the fun facts about bees and emperor penguins come together in the end. It is a very inspiring essay to read and very relatable as well. I also grew up watching so many shows about families like Full House or Modern Family and wished for a family like the ones I saw on the screen. You successfully exhibited to the audience the reality of parenthood and your concerns about your ability to be a father.

annotations.text: It is a very inspiring essay to read and very relatable as well. I also grew up watching so many shows about families like Full House or Modern Family and wished for a family like the ones I saw on the screen.
annotations.comment: This is a really interesting point with good details--I would love to see you explore this more, maybe make a quick comparison between your own family and one from tv--could you discuss what you wished was different about your own folks?

paragraphs.body: While reading the essay I wondered how did you come up with the idea of using the alphabet? Was it to keep the audience on their toes or to confuse? Once again, it helped to keep the tone not too serious and was very interesting. Thank you for writing this essay and reminding your readers that writing does not have to be conventional, I would like to be as creative as you when it comes to writing in the future.

annotations.comment: This is some really good writing. You're doing a great job of incorporating academic elements of writing into a personal letter in a way that is at once seamless and convincing. I think Dinty would really be honored by the skill and thoughtfulness of this letter. Keep it up!
